title: Explaining tactical shift: interpretive changes of political opportunity and strategic interaction in Hong Kong's pro-democracy movement, 2010 to 2019
abstract: This article investigates what prompts social movements to modify their tactics. While previous studies suggest contextual changes trigger these shifts, they overlook individual strategic decision-making. This study proposes an analytical approach combining the interactionist perspective and insights from studies of perceived opportunities. It explains how activists' ongoing interactions with others and their targets shape their political opportunity perception and influence their strategic decisions. The article uses the Hong Kong pro-democracy movement as a case study, highlighting how factional disputes within the movement affected strategic choices, ultimately shaping the trajectory of tactical shifts.
